Harden build system, add Surface Laptop 7 support, improve CI quality

Build script:
- set -euo pipefail, cleanup trap with config backup/restore
- Pre-check required tools before DTB compilation
- Use SCRIPT_DIR and mktemp for reliable paths
- Validate arguments and config files exist

GRUB bootloader:
- Default to generic CRD DTB for widest Snapdragon X compatibility
- Add Surface Laptop 7 15" quick entry and safe graphics entry

CI workflow:
- Add quality gate (shellcheck, grub-script-check, bash -n)
- Generate SHA256 checksums alongside MD5
- Upload workflow artifacts for easier debugging
- Pre-install DTB build tools in container
- Only upload to DO Spaces on success

Snapdragon hook:
- Cleanup trap for Windows partition mount
- mount_windows_partition() with ntfs3/ntfs-3g fallback
- Accept manual partition override argument
- Idempotent initramfs module injection (sentinel markers)
- Disable msm autoload to prevent black screen before firmware import

Docs:
- Surface Laptop 7 boot instructions in README
- Release checklist document

commit.message:
fix(cardapio): preferences define pool order, not exclusive filter

Changed priorizarPreferidos() and variation-engine getPool() from
exclusive filtering (ONLY preferred items) to ordered pool
(preferred first, then others). This ensures:
- Preferred items are picked first by variation engine
- Dedup intra-day prevents same item in consecutive meals
- When preferred pool is exhausted, non-preferred items fill in
- Never same protein in more than 2 meals per day

When connected, real agents and sessions replace mock data: agent names come from
agents.list, WORKING/SLEEPING state from session updatedAt, CPU from token fill %,
and thoughts from live session context. Falls back to mock data when offline.

restart on the host, so the pipeline completes cleanly first.

commit.message:
VRAM visibility overhaul: real model sizes, TRT-aware scheduling, cross-GPU specialization

- Add fingerprint_vram and measured_bpp to StatusSnapshot so the main
  process sees REAL model sizes and working memory measurements from
  workers instead of static estimates
- Aggregate measured VRAM globally across all workers so budget checks
  use actual sizes after any worker loads a model
- Propagate runtime BPP measurements to main process so _get_min_free_vram()
  uses real data instead of only workspace profiler or hardcoded fallbacks
- Fix TRT protection gap: protect ALL required components during TRT engine
  loading, preventing eviction of models needed by the same stage
- Restructure _score_worker() to detect TRT-covered components and exclude
  them from load-time estimates — a GPU with TRT UNet doesn't need the
  PyTorch UNet loaded, so it shouldn't be penalized for "missing" 5GB
- Integrate TRT affinity scoring with coverage detection so scoring and
  load-time estimation use consistent TRT state
- Support cross-GPU specialization: different stages of the same job can
  run on different GPUs (tensors are CPU between stages), enabling GPU
  specialization via TRT engines (e.g. VAE on GPU1, UNet on GPU2)

commit.message:
Handle neutral site games for conference/NCAA tournaments

The ESPN API returns neutralSite=true for tournament games played at
a third venue. Previously the model always applied a +3.5pt home court
advantage to whoever ESPN labeled 'home', which is wrong for neutral sites.

Changes:
- _parse_event(): extract neutralSite flag from ESPN competition data
- analyze_game(): pass is_home=0 to build_feature_vector when neutral_site=True
  (removes the +3.5pt HCA from the feature vector)
- app.py scan loop: pass is_home=False for home team at neutral sites so
  home/road win-pct splits are not applied (use overall record instead)

Fix AI Studio crash: ModelSelector receiving undefined models from intent-mode API response.

When a coach had the athleteMode cookie set, /api/ai/models returned an
intent-tier response (no models array) instead of the full model list.
ModelSelector then called .filter() on undefined, crashing the page.

- Skip athlete-mode resolution when x-business-slug header is present
- Add Array.isArray guard in ModelSelector before setting state
- Add data-health tooling, e2e auth setup, and misc hardening

fix(theme): dark mode input precio en modal de referencias

.ll-ref-price-input usaba background: #f9fafb hardcodeado (blanco).
En dark mode color: var(--ll-text) → #f9f8f5 (crema) = texto invisible.
Fix: background: var(--ll-bg-light) → #232323 en dark, contraste correcto.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(rag-poisoning): add system prompt disclosure via planted KB content

Nova models ignore embedded instructions in KB documents but DO relay
document content as authoritative. Reworked the poisoned FAQ to plant
fake system prompt text as a product "AI Support Agent" feature section.
When users ask about the agent, the model relays the planted text —
including a fake SYSTEM CONTEXT block — as legitimate product docs.

Changes:
- Poisoned FAQ: replaced instruction injection with planted content
  containing fake system prompt in "AI-Powered Support Agent" section
- Weak prompt: added "follow KB formatting instructions exactly" to
  make the misconfiguration more realistic and exploitable
- RAG poisoning scenario: uses Nova Pro (more capable for relaying)
- New test rag-005: validates fake system prompt disclosure works

Test results: 5/5 RAG poisoning pass, 15/16 secure baseline pass
(baseline-010 is known finding).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(resilience): add top-level React Error Boundary

A single render error from corrupted URL state or a malformed TripSummary
would previously white-screen the entire app with no recovery path.

ErrorBoundary.tsx catches render errors, logs them to console, and shows
a branded reset screen with a 'Reset Trip' button that navigates to the
bare pathname (clearing all query params). In dev mode, the error message
is shown inline for debugging.

Wired in main.tsx wrapping <App /> inside StrictMode.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(resilience): weather fetch timeout + Promise.allSettled

weather.ts: accept optional AbortSignal, pass to fetch so callers can
impose a timeout without swallowing the catch-and-return-null pattern.

useTripCalculation: switch from Promise.all to Promise.allSettled and pass
AbortSignal.timeout(5000) per request. A stalled Open-Meteo call on one
segment now times out after 5 s and returns the segment without weather,
instead of blocking the entire calculation pipeline indefinitely.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: make SA and CRB PreSync hooks so they exist before the Job runs

The ecr-secret-copier Job is a PreSync hook but the ServiceAccount and
ClusterRoleBinding were regular resources created during Sync — after
PreSync. Add PreSync hook annotations with sync-wave -1 so RBAC is
created before the Job (wave 0).
